_meta.description: Home daycare (garde en milieu familial) regulations, permit requirements, and the STA income support program. Covers RSG (responsable de service de garde) recognition, CPE affiliation, and independent operation.

title_en: Home Daycare — Recognition as RSG (Responsable de service de garde)
title_fr: Garde en milieu familial — Reconnaissance comme RSG
plain_language_summary: Running a home daycare in Quebec is one of the most regulated micro-businesses. You need to be officially recognized as an RSG (responsable de service de garde) by a coordinating office (bureau coordonnateur). This takes 2-6 months and involves training, a home inspection, and a criminal record check. BUT — once recognized, you benefit from government subsidized rates, access to the $10/day system, and potentially the STA income support program during your startup period.

two_pathways.name_en: Affiliated Home Daycare (RSG affiliated with a Bureau Coordonnateur)
two_pathways.name_fr: RSG affiliée à un bureau coordonnateur (BC)
two_pathways.plain_language: The most common path. You operate your home daycare through a coordinating office (like a CPE) that handles administration, subsidized rates, and support. Parents pay the reduced $10-$12/day rate and the government pays you the rest.

who_qualifies: Quebec residents (citizen, permanent resident, or valid work permit)
Physically and mentally fit to care for children
Home meets safety and space requirements
No serious criminal record (certain convictions are automatic disqualifiers)
who_does_not_qualify: Certain criminal convictions (assault, child endangerment, sexual offences — permanent disqualifiers)
Homes that cannot meet childproofing and space requirements
People on certain immigration status types (verify with specific bureau coordonnateur)
eligibility.future_eligibility: If you're currently on a work permit, you may qualify depending on your specific permit type. Permanent residents qualify fully. If your criminal record contains minor convictions, review with the bureau coordonnateur — not all convictions disqualify.

capacity.with_assistant: Up to 9 children if you hire a qualified assistant
capacity.note: Your own children under 9 years old count toward the 6-child maximum

steps.title: Contact your local Bureau Coordonnateur (BC)
steps.detail: Find the BC in your borough and express interest. They will explain their specific requirements and current capacity for new RSGs in your area. Some BCs have waiting lists.

steps.title: Complete required training
steps.detail: Complete the Attestation d'études collégiales (AEC) in Early Childhood Education OR equivalent recognized training. Some BCs accept equivalent experience. Check with your BC.
steps.duration: Varies — AEC is several months; prior ECE training may be recognized
steps.cost: Varies — may be subsidized or free through Emploi-Québec

steps.title: Home inspection
steps.detail: A BC representative visits your home to ensure it meets the safety, space, and sanitation requirements of the Educational Childcare Act regulations.
checklist: Smoke detectors and carbon monoxide detectors on every floor
Secure outdoor play area (or access to one)
Adequate space per child (at least 2.8 m² per child in play area)

steps.title: Criminal record check
steps.detail: All adults living in or regularly present in the home must submit a criminal record check (Vérification des antécédents judiciaires). Some positions also require a child protection registry check.
steps.cost: Varies by municipality — typically $25-$75 for criminal record check
steps.note: Contact your local police service for a criminal record check

steps.title: First aid certification
steps.detail: RSG must hold valid pediatric first aid certification.

steps.title: Receive recognition and sign agreement with BC
steps.detail: Once all requirements are met, the BC issues your recognition and you sign a service agreement. You can begin accepting children.

compensation.how_paid: The BC collects parent contributions and government subsidies and pays you a daily rate per child
compensation.approximate_daily_rate_per_child: Approximately $45-$60/day per child (government subsidy + parent contribution)
compensation.note: Verify current rates with your BC — rates are set by the government and updated periodically

sta_program.name: Soutien au travail autonome (STA) — Income Support During Startup
sta_program.suspension_note: ⚠️ The STA program has been suspended since May 1, 2024 for an indefinite period. It is not currently available. Monitor Emploi-Québec for updates: https://www.emploiquebec.gouv.qc.ca
sta_program.plain_language: When active, this program provided income replacement while setting up your business — valuable for new RSGs building their client base. Currently suspended as of May 1, 2024.

two_pathways.name_fr: Garde en milieu familial non subventionnée
two_pathways.plain_language: You can also operate independently without a BC affiliation. Parents pay your full rate directly (typically $30-$60/day). You're not part of the $10/day system but have more flexibility in rates and operations. Still requires recognition from the MFA.

eligibility.note: Similar requirements to affiliated RSG — recognition is still required for more than 6 visits/year by the same children

two_pathways.tradeoff: Higher potential daily rate but no guaranteed government income, no BC administrative support, must find your own clients

sta_program_detail.name: Soutien au travail autonome (STA)
sta_program_detail.plain_language: ⚠️ SUSPENDED as of May 1, 2024 — indefinitely. When active, this program provided weekly income support while starting a self-employed business, and was highly relevant for new RSGs building their daycare client base. Not currently available.

cost_summary.training: Free to $2,000 depending on prior education and subsidies available
cost_summary.home_modifications: Varies — budget $200-$1,000 for safety equipment (gates, detectors, etc.)
cost_summary.note: Many costs may be subsidized through Emploi-Québec if you qualify for STA

timeline.total: 2-6 months from first contact to first child

by_step.training: Varies — may be recognized immediately if you have ECE background

common_mistakes: Starting to care for children before receiving official recognition — illegal and can result in permanent disqualification
Not knowing about the STA program — many new RSGs lose income during the startup period unnecessarily
Not budgeting for the cost of home modifications required by the inspector
Not contacting the BC early enough — some areas have waiting lists for new RSG spots
Forgetting that your own children under 9 count toward the 6-child maximum

support_resources.name: Ministère de la Famille — RSG Information

government_body: Ministère de la Famille du Québec
